findings.description: Header and footer elements are inserted out of order before the main document title.
findings.evidence: In the HTML, the bottom page address footer and a duplicate of the top-right official names block appear before the 'PROCLAMATION' title, disrupting the logical reading order.

findings.description: Duplicate footer at the end of the document.
findings.evidence: The HTML shows the 'State of North Carolina...' address block rendered twice consecutively at the bottom of the page.

findings.description: Page headers and footers from the PDF are improperly injected directly into the middle of an ordered list, disrupting the content flow and document structure.
findings.evidence:
In the HTML, between list items VI.C and VI.D, a block containing `
...State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries...
` and `
...Proclamation PT-2-2024
Pg. 2...
` is nested inside the `
  • ` for Queens Creek. A similar injection occurs between items IX.E and X.
